summary: Histoplasma capsulatum grows primarily as hyphal filaments below human body temperature but switches to yeasts when exposed to higher warmth like those found internally during infection. Thus, it meets the criteria specified by existing as yeast at 37 degrees Celsius while being present as hyphae around 25 degrees Celsius. To be precise about options provided would require additional context or choices including H. capsulatum specifically mentioned earlier findings support its unique thermally responsive morphological behavior accurately reflecting described conditions.
